hotspots.title: Israel intercepts Gaza aid flotilla, detains Italians
hotspots.blurb: Israel intercepted a Gaza-bound aid flotilla in international waters, detaining over 175 activists, including 24 Italians. Italy's government, led by PM Giorgia Meloni, condemned the action and demanded the immediate release of the detained Italians.
hotspots.affect: This event commands disproportionate attention due to its high moral charge and the involvement of international actors, sparking outrage and calls for diplomatic intervention.

hotspots.title: PP-Vox ‘national priority’ pacts in Spain
hotspots.blurb: The PP and Vox have formalized 'national priority' agreements in several Spanish regions, including Andalusia and Aragón. These pacts prioritize Spanish nationals in public services and housing, sparking controversy and accusations of discriminatory policies.
hotspots.affect: This development is highly charged due to its polarizing nature, with critics framing it as a moral and ethical issue regarding immigration and national identity.

hotspots.title: Magyar’s EU funds deal and Sovereignty Office abolition
hotspots.blurb: Hungary's new Prime Minister Péter Magyar announced plans to abolish the Sovereignty Protection Office and secure the release of frozen EU funds. This move is seen as a significant shift from the policies of former PM Viktor Orbán.
hotspots.affect: The abolition of the Sovereignty Office and the negotiation for EU funds carry high affective investment due to their implications for Hungary's governance and international relations.

hotspots.title: Trump administration’s Iran war costs and strategies
hotspots.blurb: The Trump administration's ongoing conflict with Iran has reportedly cost $25 billion, with Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth facing tough questions from lawmakers about the war's strategy and financial implications.
hotspots.affect: The high financial and human costs of the Iran conflict, along with the political contention it generates, make this a dominant theme in right-wing news coverage.

hotspots.title: PiS internal feud over Łukasz Mejza’s expulsion
hotspots.blurb: Poland's ruling PiS party expelled MP Łukasz Mejza following controversies, including traffic violations and plans to participate in a freak fight event. This has sparked internal debates and highlighted divisions within the party.
hotspots.affect: The expulsion of Mejza and the ensuing internal party strife carry high affective investment due to the moral and ethical questions raised and the implications for PiS's unity.
